mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals labor market normalization, which boosts consumer confidence and equity risk appetite, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 Rank IC over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic expansion, which increases corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 55% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index reading above 108 indicates manufacturing capacity utilization is near peak levels, which historically precedes margin compression and earnings downgrades, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive or neutral in over 65% of instances where INDPRO sustains levels above 108, or the signal exhibits no directional skew in a bootstrap test.

mechanism_narrative: Real gross domestic product growth exceeding the historical 2.5 percent annualized trend provides a fundamental earnings floor that supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns across all market conditions over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 60-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive skew when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5 percent annualized, or if the signal's information ratio falls below 0.1 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.5 percent triggers inflation hedging flows and anticipates tighter monetary policy, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered episodes, or the cross-sectional Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in consumer credit outstanding and employment growth validates sustained household spending power, which lifts retail and consumer discretionary earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive alpha in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for equity volatility indices.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between accelerating real GDP growth and stabilizing core inflation signals a healthy demand-supply balance that supports corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon when both conditions align.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month change < 0.3%
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in over 55% of triggered instances, or if the combined signal's predictive R-squared drops below 0.02 in a rolling 2-year window.
